rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM A. WINEBERG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 24, 2011, line 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSHUA JENNINGS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. As to the 1st and 2nd causes of action (violation of due process under the California and United States Constitutions), the Court finds that the Ordinance is not Constitutionally vague. See Ford Dealers Assn. v. Dept. of Motor Vehicles, (1982) 32 Cal.3d 347; Habitat Trust for Wildlife v. City of Rancho Cucamonga, (1009) 15 Cal.app.4th 1306. Futhermore, ¿while an enactment may be redundant, it is not vague.¿ ACLU v. Bd. Of Education, (1963) 59 Cal.2d 203. Plaintiff¿s facial challenge to the predeprivation review also fails. Love v. Monterey, (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 580; People v. Amor, (1974) 12 Cal.3d 20, Roth v. Los Angeles, (1975) 53 Cal.App.3d 679. As long as a predeprivation review is provided by the agency administering the statute, the statute is valid even if the statute does not expressly provide for it. Moreover, the record clearly shows evidence that the review is adequate under Mackey v. Montryum (1979) 443 U.S. 1 Plaintiff¿s claims under the 3rd cause of action concerning the notice and the penalties assessed against him are moot. Plaintiff¿s arguments that the notice fails to comply with Section 3712 and 3715 was never pled in the complaint or amended complaint and cannot be raised at this time. Plaintiff¿s claim under the 5Th cause of action regarding the consideration of evidence outside of the record is also moot and the evidence is cleaR that there is no reason to believe that the hearing officer will make that mistake again. Based on the above, Plaintiff is not entitled to declaratory or injunctive relief. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ATHLONE DEVELOPMENT LLC, et al VS. EDISON MALO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 24, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT WELL FARGO BANK, N.A. DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUND OF DEFECTIVE PLEADING (COMBINING PLAINTIFFS) IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED IS OVERRULED AS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DETERMINATION AT THE PLEADING STAGE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS RE: OBJECTIVE INDICIA FROM WHICH THE BANK COULD REASONABLY CONCLUDE THAT THE PARTY PRESENTING THE CHECK IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO TRANSACT IN MANNER PROPOSED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ATHLONE DEVELOPMENT LLC, et al VS. EDISON MALO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 24, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT BOREL PRIVATE BANK & TRUST COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS RE: OBJECTIVE INDICIA. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ATHLONE DEVELOPMENT LLC, et al VS. EDISON MALO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 24, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT WELL FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPHS 21-26 IS DENIED. THE QUESTION OF WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED RELIES ON A QUESTION OF FACT AS TO WHEN THE BANK STATEMENTS WERE AVAILABLE WHICH IS NOT PROPERLY DETERMINED AT THIS PLEADING STAGE OF THE CASE. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPHS 27-44 IS ALSO DENIED. WHETHER OR NOT PLAINTIFF SHOULD HAVE REASONABLY DISCOVERED THE UNAUTHORIZED PAYMENTS IS A QUESTION OF FACT NOT TO BE DETERMINED AT THE STAGE IN THE PROCEEDINGS. =(302/LMG)
